description_long: This is a three-story real estate agency building with a classic European architectural style, featuring two distinct sections joined together. The facade is adorned with large, rectangular windows arranged symmetrically, and the ground floor includes two main entrances. The left section has a steep, dark gray tiled roof with a small chimney, while the right section features a similarly steep roof with a different tile pattern and a dormer. The rear and side walls are plain, with minimal detailing and no windows, emphasizing the building's urban placement. The exterior is finished in light-colored plaster with subtle weathering, and the base is slightly darker, suggesting a stone or concrete foundation. The overall structure is solid and imposing, typical of older city buildings repurposed for commercial use.
description: A three-story, light-plastered real estate agency building with large windows, steep tiled roofs, and dual entrances, reflecting classic European urban architecture.
description_view_0: Front view showing two main entrances, large symmetrical windows, and both roof styles.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying a plain, windowless wall and the sloped roof with a chimney.
description_view_2: Rear view with minimal detailing, brickwork on the upper left, and no visible entrances.
description_view_3: Right side view showing a plain wall and the full expanse of the patterned roof tiles.
